approach: Fail-closed acquisition of the order-42 control corpus through a new mirror path, plus full-adjacency parser hardening

strategy.mechanism: A frozen source-byte preflight gates complete upper-triangle adjacency agreement across two custom parsers and NetworkX, two exact forbidden-set evaluators, nauty complementation, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: The Hugging Face resolve path can supply the frozen corpus bytes, and complete adjacency fingerprints can close the remaining parser-equivalence gap before any target evaluation.
discriminating_test: Attempt one bounded mirror download; require 47,888 bytes and the frozen SHA-256 before target work; independently require all three parsers to agree on complete adjacency for every order-7 class and complement.
summary: The new Hugging Face acquisition path failed DNS resolution with curl code 6 and left no corpus or HTTP headers. The final target preflight returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false. The gate was strengthened from order/edge/degree signatures to complete upper-triangle adjacency fingerprints across Python, C, and NetworkX; the final exhaustive order-7 regression passed on 1,044 unlabeled classes and their complements, and the semantic/parser controls also passed. No order-42 record was checked, no canonical novelty comparison ran, and no Ramsey bound changed.
rationale: The acquisition failure keeps the strategy blocked, but the complete-fingerprint requirement removes a concrete verifier weakness that coarse signatures could miss. The positive evidence is a reusable research-software improvement with an exhaustive finite test only at order 7.
claims: Complete upper-triangle adjacency fingerprints agree across the Python parser, C parser, and NetworkX on all 1,044 unlabeled order-7 graph classes and their 1,044 complements.
The same suite has exact checker-output agreement and local complement bytes agree with nauty-complg.
The provisional K43 semantic-mutation, malformed-input, violation-identity, and deletion controls still pass after fingerprint hardening; publisher authentication remains false.
Zero order-42 corpus records were checked and the rigorous range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-210542-55792e: mirror acquisition returned curl code 6 and left no source file
records/delegate-provenance-epoch3.json records the exact promoted Terra memo and Sol audit
next_steps: Do not retry ANU or Hugging Face curl in the unchanged command environment.
Obtain the unchanged McKay bytes through a genuinely new raw-file export path or a user-supplied file with retrieval provenance.
Require exactly 47,888 bytes, 328 nonblank LF-terminated records, and SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb.
Run the final authenticated-corpus command in CHECKPOINT.md; stop at the first complete-fingerprint or mathematical mismatch.
Only after that gate passes, authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 before any K43 repair.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-210542-55792e: Hugging Face acquisition failed with curl code 6
.proof-experiments/20260720-210716-8da301: semantic mutations and parser-rejection controls passed
.proof-experiments/20260720-210833-0111b6: final target preflight returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false
independent_checker: Checker A uses Python, a Boolean adjacency matrix, and literal 5-subset enumeration. Checker B uses C, a separate graph6 parser, and recursive bitset clique enumeration. Both emit complete upper-triangle bits; NetworkX independently decodes and emits the same bits, and nauty-complg checks complement bytes. None ran on order 42 because the source file was absent.
transfer_insights: Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> coarse graph signatures can hide decoder errors -> complete adjacency fingerprints agreed across three parsers on the exhaustive order-7 control suite.
Fail-closed provenance -> missing bytes must prevent mathematical evaluation -> the final target preflight recorded downstream_checks_run=false.

established_facts.claim: Complete upper-triangle adjacency fingerprints from three parser paths agree on every unlabeled order-7 graph class and complement.
established_facts.scope: 1,044 source representatives and 1,044 complements at order 7 only

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Retry direct curl acquisition from ANU or Hugging Face in the unchanged command environment.
ruled_out.reason: Both hosts fail DNS resolution before any HTTP response.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A new network/export route or user-supplied raw file with provenance becomes available.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start order-42 novelty or K43 repair search from the current packet.
ruled_out.scope: Any continuation lacking authenticated McKay and Springer bytes
ruled_out.reason: The source preflight stopped before every target-instance check.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The McKay corpus passes the complete-fingerprint gate and the Springer seed then passes its raw-source gate.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay records and their 328 complements.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty and canonical-comparison claim depends on these controls.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes with retrieval provenance at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the exact command in CHECKPOINT.md.

open_leads.description: Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 only after the corpus passes.
open_leads.rationale: It is the strongest published bounded-repair seed, but the local transcription lacks publisher provenance.
open_leads.next_experiment: Retrieve the unchanged TXT, determine its color convention, and require exact violation identities under both checkers.

blocker: Neither tested command-host resolves, browser retrieval cannot export the raw body, and no authenticated order-42 corpus exists locally.
reopen_condition: Provide or retrieve unchanged McKay bytes with provenance matching the frozen size, record count, and SHA-256 discriminator.

continuation.objective: Close the authoritative order-42 corpus control gate.
continuation.first_action: Do not retry the current curl paths. Supply sources/r55_42some.g6 through a genuinely new raw-file path, preserve retrieval metadata, and run the command in CHECKPOINT.md.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop at any provenance, byte, record, full 861-bit parser fingerprint, forbidden-set, histogram, degree, complement, or canonical-label mismatch.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can later R(5,5) computations depend on a fail-closed control gate that detects every adjacency-level parser disagreement?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The prior gate compared only order, edge count, and ordered degrees across its third parser; this version compares all adjacency bits and will retain per-record adjacency hashes.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: The unchanged authoritative corpus passes the final gate from a cold start and the report receives an independent external replay.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this artifact adds fail-closed multi-parser verification and claims no new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey DS1.18 and maintained corpus index
novelty_searches.query: current R(5,5) range and r55_42some corpus status
novelty_searches.finding: The exact value remains open and the maintained corpus is explicitly not a complete classification.

external_channel.acceptance_path: No contact or submission until the authoritative raw-source replay succeeds.

independent_validations.validator: NetworkX 3.3 and nauty 2.8.8+ds-5 local exhaustive regression
independent_validations.result: Complete adjacency agreement and byte-exact complement agreement on all 2,088 order-7 source/complement records

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. Two precomputed GPT-5.6 Terra memos were advisory; only the experiment-verification memo supplied a relied verifier requirement, was hash-verified, promoted exactly into docs/delegate-memos, and independently implemented and tested by Sol. Deterministic tools used: Python 3.12.3, C/GCC, nauty 2.8.8+ds-5, NetworkX 3.3, curl, SHA-256, Git diagnostics, the computational-researcher experiment harness, and web browsing. No new subagent, SAT solver, proof assistant, or cloud job was used.
